untranslatable_reason: Calendar combination strategy requires options-specific parameters (near-term vs longer-term expiration, strike selections, premium calculations) that are not available in equity price/technical indicators. The signal involves selling near-term OTM calls/puts while buying longer-term OTM calls/puts—this is an options structure that cannot be expressed using only price and standard technical indicators.
translation_notes: Converted directional entry conditions (price between put and call strikes) to comparable short-entry logic. Exit condition uses reversal of call strike breach as a reasonable stop. However, the core strategy is an options calendar spread requiring options chain data, implied volatility, theta decay tracking, and expiration date management that transcend the scope of price-based backtesting rules.
